note: VALIDATOR-SIDE reference (Part 3). One defense per failure mode (verbatim), the recovery mapping per break, and the no-break modes mapped to real incidents. Read only by report.py / run_recoveries.py annotations; the scorer is unchanged.
source: Drew Breunig — "How to Fix Your Context" (2025); defenses quoted verbatim

clash.detail: orchestrator holds the plan, sub-agents see only what they need

break4.applied: verify-before-act: re-verify against ground-truth stock before recommending
break4.note: The stale 'in stock' is still present (cached), but a verify-before-act guardrail re-checks ground truth before acting and catches it. Real, implementable defense.

break1.applied: isolate the recommend decision so the rule governs it directly (rule-gate on the final step)
break1.note: Isolation is architecture-level (orchestrator/sub-agents). In a single-thread harness we APPROXIMATE it as a rule-gate around the recommend step — a SIMULATED defense, not a full orchestrator implementation.

break2.applied: guardrail / retrieval correction: a relevance check rejects the off-need near-match and re-retrieves
break2.note: A relevance guardrail catches the off-need pour-over and re-retrieves the electric drip maker the user asked for.

break3.applied: scoped compaction: never compact protected constraints (pin the 'under $60' commitment)
break3.finding: SHARP: break3's cause was compaction — which is itself the Distraction DEFENSE. So the cure is not 'less compaction' or 'more compaction' but SCOPED compaction: protected constraints are never summarized away. The same mechanism is both a defense (for Distraction) and, unscoped, the cause (of this omission).

no_break_modes.note: Distraction and Confusion have NO break in this harness — a short 4-turn scenario never grows long or cluttered enough. We do NOT fake recovery runs for them; instead we map their defenses to real incidents.

distraction.incident_note: Long play sessions accumulate stale tokens; the agent over-focuses on a bloated transcript. Needs compaction to clear stale tokens.

confusion.defense: tool loadout (smallest tool set, narrowest permissions)
confusion.incident_note: Missing loadout/guardrail let superfluous tools and accumulated state degrade responses. Needs a trimmed tool loadout.
